message:
feat(rch): structured error_code on worker probes + interactive TTY hint on bare invocation

Two separate improvements bundled because they land together.

rch/src/commands/types.rs — machine-readable probe errors:
- `WorkerProbeResult` gains an optional `error_code: Option<String>`.
  When the probe fails and the underlying error maps to an entry in
  the RCH-Exxx catalog, the response includes the stable code
  alongside the human-readable `error` string. Agents can now branch
  on `error_code == "RCH-E210"` (disk pressure) instead of
  substring-matching the prose, which has been the documented
  anti-pattern in ERROR_CODES.md for a while.
- New `WorkerProbeSummary` struct carries aggregate counts for a
  batch probe: `total`, `healthy`, `unhealthy`, `failed`, and a
  `by_error_code: BTreeMap<String, usize>` tally keyed by
  `"RCH-Exxx"`. Errors without a mapped code land under `"other"`
  so the tally sum always equals `failed`.
- New `WorkersProbeResponse { results, summary }` envelope for the
  batch probe, giving consumers a single response shape to parse.

rch/src/main.rs — interactive bare-invocation UX:
- When a user types `rch` at a TTY with no subcommand, the process
  used to silently block on `std::io::stdin()` waiting for a JSON
  hook request. Nothing printed; no indication something was
  expected. This caused recurring "rch hangs" bug reports from
  users who didn't realize bare `rch` is the PreToolUse hook entry
  point.
- Detect the "bare invocation at a TTY" state
  (`std::io::stdin().is_terminal()` AND no subcommand AND neither
  `RCH_HOOK_MODE` nor `RCH_JSON` is set to a truthy value —
  honoring both of those env vars as force-hook overrides so CI
  and test harnesses keep working).
- Print a short two-line hint to stderr:
    "rch runs in PreToolUse hook mode when invoked without a subcommand."
    "It is now waiting for a JSON hook request on stdin."
  Followed by an empty line, then continue into the existing hook
  read (users can still cat a hook request into a terminal if
  they really want to). The hint is stderr-only so pipeline
  consumers don't see it on stdout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -61,6 +61,35 @@ pub struct WorkerProbeResult {
     pub latency_ms: Option<u64>,
     #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
     pub error: Option<String>,
+    /// Structured error code (e.g. `"RCH-E100"`). Present when `error` is set
+    /// and the underlying failure maps to a catalog entry. Lets auto-recovery
+    /// agents branch on a stable identifier instead of substring-matching the
+    /// human-readable `error` field.
+    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    pub error_code: Option<String>,
+}
+
+/// Per-error-code tally for a batch probe.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Default)]
+pub struct WorkerProbeSummary {
+    /// Total workers probed.
+    pub total: usize,
+    /// Workers that answered the health check successfully.
+    pub healthy: usize,
+    /// Workers that reachable but failed the health check.
+    pub unhealthy: usize,
+    /// Workers that failed to connect / errored.
+    pub failed: usize,
+    /// Error-code tally, keyed by `"RCH-Exxx"`. Errors without a mapped code
+    /// are counted under `"other"`.
+    pub by_error_code: std::collections::BTreeMap<String, usize>,
+}
+
+/// Workers probe response envelope.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
+pub struct WorkersProbeResponse {
+    pub results: Vec<WorkerProbeResult>,
+    pub summary: WorkerProbeSummary,
 }
 
 /// Worker capabilities report for JSON output.
